sections.heading: Atlas Embedding and Reranking API
sections.body:
Atlas bills the Embedding and Reranking API based on your usage:

- For text-based models, charges apply per token.

- For multimodal models that process images and videos, charges apply
  per pixel.

Usage charges for the Embedding and Reranking API appear in the
**Summary by Service** section of your invoice under
**AI Models**.

To learn more, see [Manage Billing for the Embedding and Reranking API](voyageai/management/billing).

sections.heading: Clusters
sections.body:
Atlas charges dedicated clusters on an hourly basis and
you pay only for the time your cluster is active. The cost depends on
factors like the region, cloud provider, and utilization (CPU, memory
and storage). This estimated price of your cluster is available
when you create or edit a cluster.

For example, if you run a `M30` cluster on AWS (Amazon Web Services) with default
settings at $0.54/hour for 24 hours a day for 30 days, you can expect
to pay roughly $388/month. If you increase your base storage to 200 GB,
your price changes to $0.65/hour, or roughly $468/month. If you switch
to a more expensive region like Sao Paulo and do not change any other
settings, your price changes to $0.98/hour, or around $705 monthly. To
learn more, see [Cluster Configuration Costs](cluster-config-costs).

Flex clusters scale with your usage, with a maximum charge of $30
per month. For more information, see [Atlas Flex Costs](atlas-flex-costs).

sections.heading: Storage
sections.body:
Atlas includes the cost of default storage in the cluster's hourly
rate. However, if you [Customize Cluster Storage](create-cluster-storage), Atlas charges for the full amount of
storage without deducting the cost of the default storage capacity.
If you enable [Cluster Auto-Scaling](cluster-autoscaling),
Atlas charges you based on the actual storage usage after you apply
auto-scaling.

sections.heading: Backups
sections.body:
For [billing](billing-backup-cloud-provider-snapshots),
Atlas charges for storage of data in your replica set, configuration
server, and  network transfer during snapshot restoration. If you
enable [Continuous Cloud Backups](billing-backup-cps-pit), Atlas
charges you according to the continuous cloud backup rates.

To learn more, see [Backups](cluster-config-costs-backup).

sections.heading: Data Transfer
sections.body:
Atlas calculates and updates data transfer costs daily based on the
cloud service provider hosting your cluster and if you transfer data
within the same region or different regions.

To learn more, see [data transfer costs](data-transfer-costs) and
[Optimize Cluster Costs](billing-optimizations-cluster-costs).

sections.heading: BI Connector
sections.body:
If you enable the Atlas BI Connector, Atlas charges as a daily uplift on the
cluster's cost, which you can view in the cluster console. The
daily rate follows sustained-usage pricing, meaning it is charged only up
to a monthly maximum.

sections.heading: Premium Features
sections.body:
The Premium Features section of your Atlas bill includes
additional features such as [Advanced Security](advanced-security), [Private Endpoints](privatelink-billing),
[Database Auditing](database-auditing), and [Online Archive](oa-for-atlas), with the costs based on usage, subscription,
or resource consumption.

To learn more, see [Additional Services](additional-services).

sections.heading: Data Federation
sections.body:
Atlas Data Federation costs include data that your federated database instances process and return.

To learn more, see [processed data](atlas-data-federation-billing).

sections.heading: Charts
sections.body:
Atlas bills Charts based on the data you transfer from the
Charts web server to web browser clients, with the first 1 GB
per month free, and additional data transfers beyond this limit at a
fixed rate.

To learn more, see [Atlas Charts Pricing](charts/pricing).

sections.heading: Support
sections.body:
Atlas charges for support based on the selected support tier.
Pricing details are available through the UI on the [support plan page](https://cloud.mongodb.com/go?l=https%3A%2F%2Fcloud.mongodb.com%2Fv2%23%2Forg%2F%3Corganization%3E%2Fsupport).
